title: Data Ownership, Integrity, Migrations, and Lifecycle Governance · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for data ownership, integrity, migrations, and lifecycle governance and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for governed data model and migration rehearsal. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.
Protect uniqueness, relationships, state invariants, transaction boundaries, and concurrency behavior at complementary layers.
Plan expand-migrate-contract changes with old/new reader and writer compatibility, backfill, verification, pause, and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Data Owner Source
steps.instruction:
Order the Data Owner Source evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Data Owner Source.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Data Owner Source assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Data Owner Source, keep Data Owner Source connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Data Owner Source has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Data Owner Source, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Data Owner Source precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Data Owner Source, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Data Owner Source, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The database containing a value owns its meaning.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Data Owner Source case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Data Owner Source case

stimulus.title: Data Reconciliation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Matching total row counts proves a migration preserved meaning.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.

options.text: Run the Data Reconciliation boundary case for governed data model and migration rehearsal, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Data Reconciliation result because visible governed data model and migration rehearsal output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Data Reconciliation.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Data Reconciliation without its stated preconditions, omit the governed data model and migration rehearsal trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Data Reconciliation for governed data model and migration rehearsal.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Data Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Data Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Data Reconciliation.
steps.why: Retrieving Data Reconciliation through the concrete governed data model and migration rehearsal case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Data Reconciliation, keep Data Reconciliation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.

hints: For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Data Reconciliation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Data Reconciliation precondition, or invariant that must hold.
